title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
The Bagginswood Inspector informs Johnson of a murder at Chimney House involving a foreign guest. Noting that an open window and external footprints were discovered at the scene, the inspector enlists Dr. Carter-Laithe to assist him, hoping to advance his career and avoid interference from the Metropolitan Police. While traveling to the manor, they pass "The Happy Cricketer" inn, where the doctor observes a mysterious stranger. Johnson provides a list of recent arrivals via train, which includes Lady Elinor, an American, a young soldier, Mr. Everleigh, Mrs. Ruff, and a bald, foreign-looking man. The inspector instructs Johnson to investigate strangers at "The Happy Wanderer" upon their return.

Upon arriving at the historic Chimney House, the group is met by a butler and led to Sir Carterham, the Earl of Carrington, who is visibly distressed. The Earl explains that a maid discovered the body in the banquet hall. When questioned about the victim's identity, the Earl appears unsettled before identifying him as Stanislaus Count. Lady Elinor Brent, the Earl's daughter, arrives to announce that George Romax is on his way.

In the banquet hall, Dr. Carter-Laithe examines the body. The inspector observes that while there are footprints on the outside steps leading to the middle window, there are no corresponding prints on the hardwood floor inside. Lady Elinor explains that the maid had already partially cleaned the floor before discovering the body. Sir Carterham expresses a desire to leave the investigation to George Romax, who is expected to arrive from Weefen Abbey.
